body.title: CLOSED: Wire web browser as comms://akien/ channel
body.result: comms://akien/ registered at UC startup; WS messages logged as comms://akien/web to channel_messages — commit 865bcdf, 825 tests pass
body.description:
Currently web browser messages go directly to the channel DB. They should route through comms://akien/ so web traffic is indistinguishable from Discord traffic at the bus level — same structure, same routing, same address.

When Akien sends from browser Tab 1, the message is wrapped as a comms://akien/web envelope and delivered to the bus. Replies addressed to comms://akien/ reach both web and Discord.

This makes the web browser 'just another comms:// channel' — like Discord, like IMAP. Akien is a device, his channels are delivery methods, the bus routes between them.

**Affected files:** devices/web_server/server.py (_api_cc_send), devices/akien/shim.py
**Design rules:** comms:// envelope shape consistent with Discord and IMAP channels; no TheIgors imports
**Scope boundary:** web→bus routing only. Not full bidirectional push to web (that's a future WebSocket enhancement). Replies still appear via channel polling.
**Test plan:** message sent from web Tab 1 appears in infra.channel_messages with author matching comms://akien/web pattern. Bus can address responses to comms://akien/.
